channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Chinese navy helicopter flies within 10 feet of Philippine patrol plane over disputed shoal
item.description: OVER THE SCARBOROUGH SHOAL, South China Sea (AP) — A Chinese navy helicopter flew within 10 feet (3 meters) of a Philippine patrol plane on Tuesday in a disputed area of the South China Sea, prompting the Filipino pilot to warn by radio: “You are flying too close, you are very dangerous.
item.dc:creator: JOEAL CALUPITAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tennessee man, self-proclaimed 'serial killer,' sentenced to 261 years in 5 separate murders
item.description: A Memphis man, who referred to himself as a "serial killer" during his trial, has been sentenced to a combined 261 years in prison after confessing to five murders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delta jet flips upside down on a snowy Toronto runway and all 80 aboard survive
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A Delta Air Lines jet flipped on its roof while landing Monday at Toronto’s Pearson Airport, but all 80 people on board survived and those hurt had relatively minor injuries, the airport’s chief executive said.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: European leaders want a say in talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine. The US is brushing them off
item.description: PARIS (AP) — European leaders insisted Monday they must have a say in international talks to end the war in Ukraine despite the clear message from both Washington and Moscow that there was no role for them as yet in negotiations that could shape the future of the continent.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Schools around the US confront anxiety over Trump's actions on immigration
item.description: In Fresno, California, social media rumors about impending immigration raids at the city's schools left some parents panicking — even though the raids were all hoaxes.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Utahns participate in nationwide Presidents' Day protests against Trump, Musk
item.description: Hundreds of Utahns gathered at the State Capitol on Monday, Feb. 17, to participate in an hours-long protest against President Donald Trump — but not everyone at the Capitol shared the same viewpoint.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan May, ABC 4

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month. Here's a look at each of the crashes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE access of recipient information: AP sources
item.description: The Social Security Administration's acting commissioner has stepped down from her role at the agency over Department of Government Efficiency requests to access Social Security recipient information, according to two people familiar with the official's departure who were not authorized to discuss t
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mexican musical legend Paquita la del Barrio dies at 77
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican musical legend Paquita la del Barrio, known for her powerful voice and fierce defense of women, died at her home in Veracruz early Monday at the age of 77.
item.dc:creator: BERENICE BAUTISTA, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a January fatal midair collision at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: WSJ asks Kennedy to cast aside 'antivax hooey' amid Texas measles outbreak
item.description: The Wall Street Journal's editorial board published on op-ed on Monday calling on Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to set aside his anti-vaccine activist positions in light of the nearly 50 measles cases that have been confirmed among mostly unvaccinated children in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What we know about CRJ-900, aircraft that flipped over in Toronto
item.description: The CRJ-900 can seat either 70 or 76 people, but the layout and basic dimensions for the craft are nearly identical no matter the capacity.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Gov. Ron DeSantis' former chief of staff James Uthmeier sworn in as Florida’s attorney general
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis on Monday named his former chief of staff, James Uthmeier as Florida’s attorney general, handing the state’s top law enforcement role to a trusted aide who has helped orchestrate and defend some of his most divisive initiatives.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Search and rescue efforts continue in Kentucky as death toll rises to 12 amid floods
item.description: Beshear confirmed two additional deaths on Monday morning, bringing the total weather-related fatalities to 12.
item.dc:creator: Camille Hantla, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams refuses to stand down amid protests
item.description: The mayor says he will run for reelection and fulfill "the mission that God put me on many years ago.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation

item.title: Martha Stewart to open new restaurant at Connecticut casino
item.description: Opening this fall, the restaurant is designed to "transport guests into Martha’s renowned 1925 country farmhouse located in Bedford, New York.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: More adults are wondering if they have autism: Tips to seek a diagnosis
item.description: More adults have sought insight on their own neurodiversity in the last decade — often after their children are diagnosed or after seeing social media posts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eagles player declines actor’s offer to pay Super Bowl fine
item.description: Actor and Eagles fan Rob McElhenny, best known for co-creating and starring in FXX’s “It’s Always Sunny in Philadelphia,” had recently offered to foot the bill for a fine incurred by Eagles player Jalyx Hunt during Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Krispy Kreme, Hulu team up for movie snack-inspired doughnut line
item.description: Krispy Kreme’s latest doughnut collection pays homage to classic movie theater treats. The bakery chain teamed up with Hulu to create doughnuts inspired by movie theater snacks like popcorn, slushies and boxed candy.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing hundreds of employees at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) just weeks after a deadly crash outside of Washington, D.C., put a spotlight on the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: Conservative activist Ed Martin, who echoed Donald Trump's baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol rioters, was nominated by the president on Monday to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington on a permanent basis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NAACP spending guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The nation’s oldest civil rights organization released its Black Consumer Advisory, a project intended to help educate Black Americans on “who’s pushing progress and who’s stuck in the past.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elderly couple unexpectedly finds love again in Rhode Island
item.description: Judy Furtado, 80, and George Denicourt, 82, have been going strong ever since the two met at the Johnston Senior Center.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: Man accused of stalking woman, creating fake marriage license
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn -- A man accused of stalking a woman and creating a fake marriage license was arrested Thursday in Memphis, Tennessee. On Jan.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nguyen, NewsNation

item.title: UCLA, South Carolina, Texas and Notre Dame are No. 1 seeded teams in first NCAA Tournament reveal
item.description: UCLA, South Carolina, Texas and Notre Dame would be the No. 1 seeds in the women's NCAA Tournament if it began now. The NCAA basketball selection committee on Sunday did its first reveal of the teams in line for the top 16 seeds.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5
